title: potentilla × subdigitata
intro: Potentilla × subdigitata, also known as the dwarf fivefinger, is a hybrid shrub belonging to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Potentilla fruticosa and Potentilla erecta. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Consuming Potentilla × subdigitata is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating skin issues, and supporting the digestive system. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying the leaves and flowers, which are then used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
